q: Who was the king of the gods in Greek mythology?
exp: Zeus ruled from Mount Olympus as god of the sky and thunder.
Zeus hurled thunderbolts; the trident belonged to Poseidon.

q: Who was the Greek god of the sea?
exp: Poseidon ruled the oceans with his trident.

q: What was the Roman name for Zeus?
exp: Jupiter was the Roman king of the gods, equivalent to Zeus.

q: Who was the Norse god of thunder, who wielded a hammer?
exp: Thor protected gods and humans with his hammer Mjölnir.

q: What is the name of Thor's hammer?
exp: Mjölnir always returned to Thor's hand after being thrown.

q: Who was the chief 'All-Father' god in Norse mythology?
exp: Odin was the god of war and wisdom, leader of the Aesir gods.

q: Who was the trickster god of Norse mythology?
exp: Loki was known for his tricks and for shape-shifting.

q: Who was the Greek goddess of love and beauty?
Who was the Greek goddess of wisdom?
exp: Athena protected the city of Athens, which bears her name.

q: Who was the Greek god of the underworld?
Which monster had snakes for hair and turned onlookers to stone?
exp: Anyone who met Medusa's gaze was turned to stone.

q: Who founded Rome, according to legend, raised by a she-wolf?
exp: The twins Romulus and Remus were suckled by a she-wolf.

q: What is the hall where warriors slain in battle went, in Norse myth?
exp: Valkyries carried the bravest fallen warriors to Valhalla.

q: What is the realm of the gods called in Norse mythology?
exp: Asgard was the home of the Aesir gods.
